commands.notes: All frontend TypeScript, TSX, CSS, and JSON source files match Prettier formatting.
Reported zero !important declarations against the ratcheted zero budget.
No matches; rg exit code 1 is the expected no-match result.
The shared shell visual contract passed after selector migration.
Light and dark semantic roles passed configured text contrast thresholds.
Full frontend ESLint check passed.
TypeScript project build passed.
Production build passed with menu, i18n, datetime, shell, page-admission, and smoke coverage prebuild gates.
No whitespace errors were reported.
Initial attempt reproduced an environment gap: backend port 8080 was offline, causing 502 and ECONNREFUSED failures; the reduced-motion-only scenario passed.

commands.command: go run ./backend/cmd/server with documented local MySQL, Redis, and workspace environment
commands.notes: Backend started against local MySQL and Redis; /api/v1/health returned HTTP 200 before rerunning smoke.
46/46 scenarios passed across login, shell, system pages, dialogs, forms, tables, compact layout, desktop, 1280, phone, and tablet viewports.

commands.command: node scripts/run-smoke-suite.mjs --host 127.0.0.1 --port 5173 --config playwright.config.ts -- tests/smoke/system/governance/cleanup-range-ui.spec.ts --grep "login-log filter time range" --workers=1
commands.notes: 1/1 date-range popup scenario passed, including shortcut copy and horizontal calendar panel geometry.
Post-smoke closeout check found that generated-module cleanup had restored seven generated snapshots from unformatted templates.

commands.command: npm run format && npm run format:check
commands.notes: Reformatted the seven generated snapshots; the final repository-wide frontend format check passed.
Formatting generated snapshots did not reintroduce generated business modules or cleanup drift.
The local external reviewer could not connect to its API and the workspace trust dialog had not been accepted; no independent approval is claimed.

commands.command: node scripts/run-smoke-suite.mjs --host 127.0.0.1 --port 5173 --cleanup-fixtures all --config playwright.config.ts -- tests/smoke/system/system-pages.spec.ts --workers=1 --grep "user and role smoke: role binding can be deferred"
commands.notes: The role-member drawer and overlaid confirmation modal completed add/remove binding end to end (1/1).

commands.command: node scripts/run-smoke-suite.mjs --host 127.0.0.1 --port 5173 --config playwright.config.ts -- tests/smoke/platform/shell-visual-contract.spec.ts --workers=1 --grep "dialogs use single-layer inputs|narrow mobile layout keeps actions dialogs|core system dialogs share modal spacing|form controls use a single visible focus ring"
commands.notes: Desktop, narrow-mobile, shared-dialog, and focus-state rendered contracts passed (4/4).
Pages 81/81, forms 4/4, IAM authorization 4/4, governance 18/18, and API 11/11 passed (118/118 total).

commands.command: npm run check:important-budget && npm run lint && npm run type-check && npm run build
commands.notes: Zero-priority budget, lint, TypeScript, all prebuild contracts, and production build passed after the modal fix.

browserEvidence.notes: Authentication console remains balanced with visible controls and no clipping.
Login form remains contained and readable without horizontal overflow.
Shell, filters, actions, table, and pagination retain the expected hierarchy.
Phone layout remains usable with collapsed filters, stacked actions, and contained table scrolling.
Tablet portrait keeps stable action and table geometry.
Tablet landscape keeps stable shell, filter, and table geometry.
Permission workbench tabs, cards, filters, and table remain aligned.

runtimeLogs: Focused Playwright scenarios captured console and page errors and reported no disallowed runtime errors after the backend was restored.
Responsive assertions covered horizontal overflow and viewport containment on phone and tablet layouts.
The final format gate was rerun after smoke cleanup and passed after reformatting seven generated snapshots.
knownGaps: Independent non-author review remains a required PR / human gate because the configured external reviewer was unavailable.
GitHub-hosted required checks, merge, and downstream foundation synchronization remain human/release gates.
